decisions.title: `recomputeTabs` already iterated `prevByType` indirectly through
decisions.detail: `recomputeTabs` already iterated `prevByType` indirectly through the in-memory branch state; we just stop discarding it on the transient-error path.

decisions.title: `ensureBranchSession` calls `ensureSession`, which is the same path
decisions.detail: `ensureBranchSession` calls `ensureSession`, which is the same path sync_tmux uses. No new tmux primitives.

decisions.title: The Panel redirect runs only when `decodedTabId` is not in the
decisions.detail: The Panel redirect runs only when `decodedTabId` is not in the current tabs and matches the bare-type set (closed-set: `claude`, `bash`).

decisions.title: (d) rapid Bash adds during sync_tmux pressure
decisions.detail: (d) rapid Bash adds during sync_tmux pressure

decisions.title: (f) S009 cap/floor enforcement still in force (Files/Git → 403, last
decisions.detail: (f) S009 cap/floor enforcement still in force (Files/Git → 403, last Claude → 409, 4th Claude → 409)

decisions.title: (g) 15-second Bash persistence sample (1 Hz, 0 phantom drops)
decisions.detail: (g) 15-second Bash persistence sample (1 Hz, 0 phantom drops)

decisions.title: D-1 — Preserve previous tab list across failed ListWindows
decisions.detail: the alternative was retrying the tmux call inside `recomputeTabs`, but that bakes timing assumptions into a function that's also called under the write lock from sync_tmux. The fall-back is read-only and self-converges within one sync cycle.

decisions.title: **D-2 — Synthesise canonical bash window when session is up but
decisions.detail: **D-2 — Synthesise canonical bash window when session is up but byType[bash] is empty**: this matches what bash.Provider.OnBranchOpen guarantees at session creation time. Without this, a freshly opened branch would show 0 Bash tabs until ensureSession runs, even though the persisted contract is "always at least one Bash".

decisions.title: **D-3 — `ensureBranchSession` in AddTab not in
decisions.detail: **D-3 — `ensureBranchSession` in AddTab not in pickNextWindowName**: the right layer is the AddTab handler, since it's the only operation that has to *commit* to a new window name. pickNextWindowName stays a pure read.

decisions.title: D-4 — Legacy URL redirect lives in `Panel`, not router
decisions.detail: the router doesn't have access to the live tab list. Doing the redirect in Panel keeps the rule next to the consumer (`activeTab`) and uses the existing decodedTabId pipeline.

decisions.title: The two-instance shared `tmp/` race (host palmux2 and dev palmux2
decisions.detail: The two-instance shared `tmp/` race (host palmux2 and dev palmux2 both managing the same set of `_palmux_*` sessions and killing each other's not-yet-tracked recoveries). This is a development-setup quirk, not a production bug. Workaround documented in `docs/development.md` (use distinct INSTANCE names + separate config dirs). Long-term fix is owner-tagging tmux sessions with the managing palmux2 PID.

decisions.title: The 3-second WS reconnect cycle the user observed (Bug 3) was the
decisions.detail: The 3-second WS reconnect cycle the user observed (Bug 3) was the symptom of the same recomputeTabs issue: every dropped Bash tab in the FE snapshot tore down the Bash terminal-view → unmount → WS close → reconnect. With (a) and (b) holding stable, the WS now stays attached. Verified by case-g (15-second 1 Hz sample, 0 phantom drops).
